topic: How to bleed a radiator
facebook:
🔧 Cold spots on your radiators? Bleed them.

How to do it:
1. Turn heating on and wait 20 mins
2. Feel each radiator — cold at the top = trapped air
3. Turn heating OFF and let it cool
4. Use a radiator key to open the bleed valve (anti-clockwise)
5. Air hisses out → water appears → close the valve
6. Check boiler pressure and top up if needed

If they're still cold after bleeding, it could be sludge — power flushing fixes that.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/radiators.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
instagram:
Cold radiators? 🥶 Here's the fix.

Step 1 — heat on, wait 20 mins, feel for cold spots at the top
Step 2 — turn heating OFF, let it cool
Step 3 — use a radiator key, open bleed valve slowly
Step 4 — air hisses out, then water = job done, close it up
Step 5 — check your boiler pressure

Still cold after bleeding? Could be sludge — drop me a message.

topic: Why your boiler pressure keeps dropping
facebook:
📉 Boiler pressure dropping below 1 bar? Here's what's happening.

Common causes:
🔴 Small leak somewhere in the system (radiators, pipes, or boiler itself)
🔴 Pressure relief valve releasing (means it's been overpressured)
🔴 Expansion vessel fault

Topping up the pressure yourself is fine short-term — but if it drops again within a week, there's an underlying fault that needs fixing.

Don't keep ignoring it. A small leak left alone causes big damage.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

topic: How to top up boiler pressure safely
facebook:
📊 Boiler pressure too low? Here's how to top it up safely.

Your boiler pressure should sit between 1–1.5 bar when cold.

1. Turn the boiler OFF and let it cool
2. Find the filling loop — usually a silver/grey braided hose under the boiler
3. Open both valves (quarter turn) until pressure reaches 1.2–1.5 bar
4. Close both valves — make sure they're fully closed
5. Turn the boiler back on

⚠️ If pressure rises above 2.5 bar, don't use the boiler — call an engineer.
⚠️ If it keeps dropping, there's a leak — call me.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

3️⃣ Open both valves until pressure hits 1.2 bar
4️⃣ Close both valves fully
5️⃣ Turn boiler back on

⚠️ Goes above 2.5 bar = don't use it, call an engineer

topic: No heating or hot water — what to check first
facebook:
❄️ No heating or hot water? Check these before calling an engineer.

✅ Boiler pressure — should be between 1–1.5 bar
✅ Timer and thermostat — is it set correctly? Has a power cut reset it?
✅ Check all radiator thermostats aren't turned to zero
✅ Frozen condensate pipe (in very cold weather — outside pipe from boiler)
✅ Check your electricity supply — boilers need power to ignite

If none of these fix it, your boiler likely needs a diagnostic. I'm available 7 days.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

topic: Power flushing — when you need it
facebook:
🚿 Is your central heating system full of sludge? Here's how to tell.

Signs you need a power flush:
❌ Some radiators take ages to warm up
❌ Cold patches at the bottom of radiators
❌ Boiler making banging or kettling noises
❌ System needs bleeding constantly
❌ Radiators warm at top but cold at bottom

Over time, heating systems build up rust, sludge, and scale. Power flushing cleans it all out — restoring efficiency and extending your boiler's life.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/power-flushing.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

✅ No more repair bills on an ageing system

topic: Dripping tap — fix it or leave it?
facebook:
💧 That dripping tap is costing you more than you think.

A tap dripping once per second wastes over 30 litres of water per day — that's 11,000 litres a year.

On a metered supply, that's around £30–£50/year wasted. For hot water? Add the energy cost on top.

Most dripping taps need a new washer or cartridge — usually a quick and inexpensive fix.

topic: Toilet keeps running — common causes
facebook:
🚽 Toilet constantly running? It's not just annoying — it's expensive.

A running toilet can waste 200–400 litres per day. On a meter, that's £100–200/year down the drain.

Common causes:
🔹 Worn flapper valve
🔹 Faulty fill valve
🔹 Float set too high
🔹 Siphon failure (older cisterns)

Most of these are quick fixes — half an hour, inexpensive parts. Don't let it run.

You get a Gas Safety Record at the end confirming everything is safe — or a list of any remedial work needed.

topic: Frozen condensate pipe — how to fix it yourself
facebook:
🥶 Boiler stopped working in cold weather? Check the condensate pipe.

Modern condensate boilers have a plastic pipe (usually white or grey) that exits outside the property. In freezing temperatures it can ice up and cause the boiler to lock out.

How to fix it yourself:
1. Find the pipe outside (usually near the boiler on an external wall)
2. Pour warm water (NOT boiling) along the frozen section
3. Reset the boiler once it thaws

If this keeps happening, the pipe may need insulating or rerouting.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
instagram:
Boiler stopped in cold weather? It might be the condensate pipe. 🥶

The plastic pipe that runs outside can freeze in winter, causing the boiler to lock out.

✅ Find the outside pipe
✅ Pour WARM water (not boiling) along it
✅ Reset the boiler

Done. Usually takes 5 minutes.

Keeps happening? It needs insulating — drop me a message.

📞 07700 155 655

googlebusiness:
In cold weather, a modern condensate boiler's external plastic pipe can freeze, causing the boiler to lock out and stop working. To fix it, pour warm water (not boiling) along the frozen section outside, then reset the boiler.

If this happens repeatedly, the pipe may need insulating or rerouting to a warmer location.

Serving Southampton and surrounding areas.

topic: Shower losing pressure — causes and fixes
facebook:
🚿 Shower pressure getting worse? Here's what's usually causing it.

For electric showers:
🔹 Limescale buildup on the flow restrictor — cleaning or replacing it usually fixes this
🔹 Failing heating element

For mixer and power showers:
🔹 Blocked or failing pump
🔹 Limescale in valves
🔹 Low mains pressure (affects the whole house)

Most shower pressure problems are fixable without replacing the whole unit.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/shower-repair.html

topic: Central heating not working — full checklist
facebook:
🏠 Central heating not working? Work through this checklist before calling.

1. Is the thermostat set above the current room temperature?
2. Is the programmer/timer set correctly?
3. Is the boiler pressure between 1–1.5 bar?
4. Is the boiler showing an error code? (look it up — most are self-explanatory)
5. Have all thermostatic radiator valves been turned to zero by mistake?
6. Is the condensate pipe frozen? (in very cold weather)
7. Has a circuit breaker tripped?

If you've checked all of these and still no heat — call me.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/central-heating.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

4️⃣ Error code on the boiler?

7️⃣ Circuit breaker tripped?

topic: Radiator replacement and system clean
facebook:
🔧 Radiator job completed today in Portswood.

Customer had a radiator that hadn't properly heated in years — cold at the bottom, warm at the top. Tried bleeding it multiple times with no improvement.

Diagnosis: heavy sludge buildup inside the panel. The radiator was replaced and the system was chemically flushed and dosed with inhibitor.

Full heat across the radiator within an hour of completing. Customer noticed the rest of the system running better too.

topic: Pipe leak — act fast to avoid damage
facebook:
💧 A small pipe leak can cause thousands of pounds of damage if ignored.

Signs you have a leak:
🔴 Damp patches on ceilings or walls
🔴 Water stains that appear and disappear
🔴 Unexplained increase in your water bill
🔴 Sound of running water when everything is off
🔴 Boiler pressure dropping regularly

The sooner a leak is found and fixed, the less damage it causes. Don't wait.
